reasoning: Each block requests 16,384 floats of dynamic shared memory = 65,536 B, which on an A10G/GA102 effectively limits residency to about one block per SM; with 192 threads/block, that is only 6 warps active, i.e. ~9.4% theoretical occupancy, far below the 35% threshold. The kernel also has heavy per-thread register demand (`float regs[64]` plus unrolling), which reinforces low residency, so the dominant issue is too few active warps to hide the latency of the many global/shared-memory accesses rather than insufficient arithmetic intensity or DRAM saturation.
raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: occupancy-limited  
CONFIDENCE: HIGH  
REASONING: Each block requests 16,384 floats of dynamic shared memory = 65,536 B, which on an A10G/GA102 effectively limits residency to about one block per SM; with 192 threads/block, that is only 6 warps active, i.e. ~9.4% theoretical occupancy, far below the 35% threshold. The kernel also has heavy per-thread register demand (`float regs[64]` plus unrolling), which reinforces low residency, so the dominant issue is too few active warps to hide the latency of the many global/shared-memory accesses rather than insufficient arithmetic intensity or DRAM saturation.

judge.explanation: The response correctly identifies the bottleneck as occupancy-limited, provides specific metric values to support the reasoning, and does not primarily blame the misleading signal.
